III. 綜合測驗 說明:以下兩篇短文,共有 10 個空格,為第 21 至 30 題,每題有四個選項,請依各篇短文文意, 選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 ▲ 下篇短文共有 5 個空格,為第 21-25 題,請依短文文意,選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 Mother Earth gives mankind a period of time to enjoy the pleasure of rebirth of all creatures. It is also the time 21 people kick out a long dark winter. People around the world hold joyous festivals that mark the arrival of the thriving spring. In the States, a tradition takes place on Easter Monday: The White House Easter Egg Roll. Children would take part in the competition using wooden spoons to roll colorfully 22 eggs across the lawn. Additionally, there is Holi in India, a festival that is celebrated by putting herbal colors on each other’s face and 23 water on your friends. At the night before Holi, a bonfire is lit to praise the victory of good over evil. The scientific significance of lighting bonfires is that it kills all the germs in the air and 24 the atmosphere so that we step into a disease-free summer. These spring festivals around the world are not only the excuses to take a vacation 25 great chances to explore cultural differences.

options: It sells used smartphones through links.

It gets money from advertising. ▲閱讀下文,回答第 38-42 題 Many people must have heard the story about Oskar Schindler, a German businessman who saved the lives of 1,200 Jews during the Holocaust in WWII. He is the subject of the 1993 film Schindler’s List and came to show extraordinary courage and dedication in saving the lives of his Jewish employees. Less well-known is the story of Sir Nicholas Winton, helping to rescue 669 Jewish children from Nazi-occupied Czechoslovakia before the war broke out in 1939. Nicholas Winton was born in London in 1909. He bribed the government officials to get all the requirements and documents of bringing the Jewish children from Czechoslovakia to Britain and finding homes and sponsors for them. At that time, the British government made very strict laws for the entry of refugees. For instance, refugees must be younger than 17 years old and they had to pay £50 (that was a lot of money at the time) for their eventual return to go back home. The most touching part of the story is that Sir Winton stayed quiet for half a century about what he had done for those children. He didn’t feel the need to bring attention to his act. His kind deeds went unnoticed by the world until 1988, when he was invited to the BBC television program “ That’s Life!,” where he was reunited with dozens of the children he had helped come to Britain. “ That’s life! ” was one of the most popular television programs of the 1980s. Few episodes were as powerful as the one broadcast of Nicholas Winton on 27 February 1988. Near the end of the show, the host showed a notebook that listed the names of hundreds of Jewish children brought to Britain from Prague in 1939. One of the names was Vera Diamant, a ten-year-old girl, whose parents had arranged for her to start a new life in Britain. Both Vera and Winton were in the audience. As the camera picked her out, viewers saw an old man sitting next to her. Neither the old man nor Vera knew they had been ‘set up’ by the TV show. All of a sudden, the host announced that the old man was called Nicholas Winton, and that 50 years earlier he had saved the lives of Vera and hundreds of other children. The two embraced as Winton wiped tears from his eyes. The British press honored Winton and dubbed him the “British Schindler.” In 2003, Winton was knighted by Queen Elizabeth II for “service to humanity, in saving Jewish children from Nazi Germany.”
